author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
prāṇeśvari! eibāra karuṇā kara more
daśanete tṛṇa dhari’, añjali mastake kari,
eijana nivedana kare

priya⋅sahacarī⋅saṅge, sevana kariba raṅge,
aṅge veśa karibeka sādhe
rākha ei sevā⋅kāje, nija pada⋅paṅkaje,
priya sahacarīgaṇa⋅mājhe

sugandhi candana, maṇimaya ābharaṇa,
kauṣika vasana nānā raṅge
ei saba sevā jā̐ra, dāsī jena haṅa tā̐ra,
anukṣaṇa thāki tā̐ra saṅge

jala suvāsita kari, ratana bhṛṅgāre bhari,
karpūravāsita guyā pāna
esaba sājāiyā ḍālā, lavaṅga, mālatī⋅mālā,
bhakṣyadravya nānā anupama

sakhīra iṅgita habe, esaba āniyā kabe,
jogāiba lalitāra kāche
narottama⋅dāsa kaya, ei jena mora haya,
dā̐ḍāiyā rahu sakhīra pāche
en_translation:
Mistress of my life! This time, please show mercy to me. Holding a blade of grass in my teeth and with folded hands upon my head, I submit this petition.

With Your beloved companions, I will joyfully perform service and eagerly dress Your body with ornaments. Engage me in this service at Your lotus feet, among Your dear companions.

Fragrant sandalwood paste, jewel-studded ornaments, and silk garments of various colors—may I become the maidservant of Her for whom all these services are performed, and always remain with Her.

Making the water fragrant and filling a jeweled pitcher, preparing betel nut flavored with camphor—arranging all these in a basket, along with cloves, a garland of jasmine flowers, and various incomparable foodstuffs.

When the companion gives a signal, when will I bring all these and offer them to Lalitā? Narottama dāsa says: may this be my destiny, to stand behind the companions.
